The Supreme Court established the right to marry in Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015).
This followed the reasoning in United States v. Windsor, 570 U.S. 744 (2013).
See also Loving v. Virginia, 388 U.S. 1 (1967). The court noted in id. at 12 that marriage
is fundamental. The defendant's argument relies on Bowers v. Hardwick, 478 U.S. 186 (1986),
which was later overruled by Lawrence v. Texas, 539 U.S. 558 (2003).
